code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase that does NOT repeat the subject.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced with the cleaned object.
    phrase_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "has {obj} floors",
        "location": "located on {obj}",
        "cost": "costs {obj}",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of {obj} square metres",
        "architect": "designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "has a height of {obj} metres",
        "buildingType": "is a {obj}",
        "developer": "developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "has a tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "has the address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "has {obj} beds",
        "region": "is in the region of {obj}",
        "state": "is in the state of {obj}",
        "website": "has website {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has government type {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "features architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
    }

    # Assume all triples share the same subject (typical for building data)
    subject = triples[0].subject
    # Prepend an article if the subject does not already start with one
    if not subject.lower().startswith(("the ", "a ", "an ")):
        subject_phrase = f"The {subject}"
    else:
        subject_phrase = subject

    # Build clause list without repeating the subject
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        obj_clean = clean_obj(triple.object)
        phrase_template = phrase_map.get(triple.predicate, "{obj}")
        clause = phrase_template.format(obj=obj_clean)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Order: place location clause last for better flow
    location_clause = None
    other_clauses = []
    for c, t in zip(clauses, triples):
        if t.predicate == "location":
            location_clause = c
        else:
            other_clauses.append(c)

    # Construct the sentence
    if other_clauses:
        body = ", ".join(other_clauses[:-1])
        if len(other_clauses) > 1:
            body += f", and {other_clauses[-1]}"
        else:
            body = other_clauses[0]
        if location_clause:
            sentence = f"{subject_phrase}, {body}, {location_clause}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject_phrase}, {body}."
    else:
        # Only location or single clause
        if location_clause:
            sentence = f"{subject_phrase} is {location_clause}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject_phrase} {clauses[0]}."

    # Capitalize first character (subject already capitalized)
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
